title: euchiton lateralis
intro: Euchiton lateralis, also known as the creeping snow tussock, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Euchiton lateralis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to help manage conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
